sections.heading: Overview
sections.content:
Global FX Overview — Asian Session

The US dollar extended its dominant run during Asian trading, with the greenback posting broad-based gains as markets continue to price in persistent Fed hawkishness amid sticky inflation concerns and robust economic data. Risk sentiment remains fragile as investors reassess the global monetary policy landscape, with carry trades unwinding across high-beta currencies while safe-haven flows bolster USD and JPY positioning.

Currency Strength Ranking

Strongest to weakest intraday: JPY (+0.31% via USD/JPY), USD (+0.42% basket), CHF (+0.18% via USD/CHF), CAD (+0.11% via USD/CAD), EUR (-0.20%), GBP (-0.30%), NZD (-0.92%), AUD (-0.93%). Japanese yen strength reflects intervention concerns near 159.00 level, while commodity currencies face dual headwinds from dollar strength and growth concerns. European currencies continue weekly declines amid ECB dovishness speculation.

FX Majors Analysis

EUR/USD 1.1639 remains under pressure, testing key support at 1.1620 with negative momentum accelerating. Weekly decline of 1.20% reflects ECB policy divergence fears and European growth concerns. Bias remains bearish below 1.1680 resistance.

GBP/USD 1.3351 extends losses, approaching critical 1.3300 support level. Sterling weakness reflects

sections.heading: Analysis
sections.content:
BoE dovish pivot expectations and UK fiscal concerns. Risk of deeper correction toward 1.3200 if support breaks.

USD/JPY 158.8760 approaches intervention zone near 159.00, with BoJ verbal warnings intensifying. Upside momentum strong but intervention risk caps advances. Critical resistance at 159.20.

AUD/USD 0.7147 leads major declines, pressured by China growth concerns and RBA dovish shift expectations. Support at 0.7120 critical; break targets 0.7080.

USD/CHF 0.7856 continues steady advance, with SNB tolerance for franc weakness supporting upside. Resistance at 0.7880, targeting 0.7920.

USD/CAD 1.3739 benefits from BoC rate cut expectations despite oil price stability. Resistance at 1.3760 key for further gains.

NZD/USD 0.5849 severely pressured by RBNZ dovish pivot and risk-off sentiment. Support at 0.5820 under threat.

FX Crosses Rotation

EUR/JPY 184.8490 consolidates near key resistance, with intervention concerns capping yen weakness. GBP/JPY 212.0990 faces similar dynamics with 213.00 resistance. AUD/JPY 113.5130 reflects broader AUD weakness, targeting 113.00 support. EUR/GBP 0.8715 shows euro resilience versus sterling, approaching 0.8730 resistance. GBP/AUD 1.8684 rebounds as AUD underperforms despite sterling weakness.

Flows and Positionin

sections.heading: Outlook
sections.content:
g

• Carry trade unwinding accelerates in AUD/JPY and NZD/JPY amid volatility surge
• European sovereign flows favoring bunds over peripheral debt, supporting EUR weakness
• Corporate USD demand remains elevated ahead of month-end fixing
• Options expiries at USD/JPY 159.00 and EUR/USD 1.1650 providing technical levels
• Real money flows rotating from commodity currencies toward USD cash positions

Intraday Trade Ideas

LONG: USD/CAD targeting 1.3760 on BoC dovish expectations; USD/CHF toward 0.7880 on momentum; GBP/AUD bounce to 1.8720 on AUD weakness.

SHORT: AUD/USD break of 0.7120 targeting 0.7080; NZD/USD below 0.5820 toward 0.5780; EUR/GBP fade of 0.8730 resistance.

Macro Drivers Today

US existing home sales and manufacturing PMI data expected to reinforce Fed hawkish bias. ECB officials speaking in European session may address recent dovish speculation. Australian wage price index could influence RBA expectations.

Key Risks

BoJ intervention risk intensifies above USD/JPY 159.00 with potential for coordinated G7 action. European political tensions around fiscal policies could accelerate EUR weakness. China stimulus speculation remains wildcard for commodity currency recovery, while US data disappointment could trigger USD profit-taking across majors.
